From July 2019 to ‌March 2026, Google's Games/Google ⁠Velocity Program, which it internally called "Project Hug", offered domestic and overseas game developers financial support for using Google ⁠services such as Cloud, Ads and YouTube, provided that they launched games on Google's app store on terms at least as favourable as ​rival app ​marketplaces, the report said.
